text:
Once upon a time in the sleepy town of Sleepsville, a big red book landed on Olive’s kitchen table one sunny afternoon. Curious, Olive opened it to a colorful cover featuring a dragon flying over a cloud forest. Inside, under an artful illustration of twinkling stars, lay a worn-out legend of bravery.

Olive remembered her grandmother telling her tales about the Brave Winged Dragon. Every night before sleep, she’d listen. “She travels through the skies to bring warmth to sleepy villages.”

Inspired, Olive decided to find this mythical creature. First, she’d visit her old friend Owlio, who always seemed wise.

“Hoot,” Owlio murmured from his perch high in the oak tree. “To find a dragon, you’d want courage.”

“You think I’ve got that?” Olive asked hopefully.

“A wise heart—but a tiny dragon fly?”

Olive shrugged. “At least start small.”
